title: Annotated visual-diff surface — shaping the contract (delta-type taxonomy · anchor payload · accept/typed-region model)
summary: Prep research for decision #2538 (graduated from #2533 Fork 5). The before/after annotated visual-diff surface — two renders of one subject (baseline vs new, or design vs built) with the divergent regions called out as numbered, typed, clickable regions carrying a per-region review verdict — is established, recurring prior art: Percy (approve/reject snapshots, promotes baseline), Chromatic (accept/deny per story + region highlights), reg-suit/reg-cli (categorizes items new/passed/changed/deleted; 2-up/swipe/blend/diff-mask UI), Playwright toHaveScreenshot (pixel diff-mask + maxDiffPixelRatio, rewrites the committed baseline), GitHub/GitLab PR diff (structural file+line hunks; per-FILE 'viewed'), Abstract (Sketch version-control, per-layer structural visual diff) and Figma branch-merge review (per-frame side-by-side). Measured against the nearest existing standard, we:src/_data/intents/audit-timeline.json — which is a TEMPORAL text/event feed of AuditEvents for one entity and CANNOT express 'these two rendered states differ HERE, and the difference is THIS kind'; the gap is real and audit-timeline is the nearest-but-non-covering neighbor. FINDING 1 (load-bearing) — delta TYPE and delta DISPOSITION are orthogonal: reg-suit's new/changed/deleted is a STRUCTURAL type (what changed about the region's existence); Chromatic/Percy's accept/deny is a REVIEW disposition (the verdict). No single incumbent first-classes BOTH (reg-suit has the type not the disposition, Chromatic/Percy the reverse, GitHub types per-hunk but approves at PR level), so the two-axis model is a synthesis ACROSS the tools; folding them into one flat enum reproduces the orthogonality break WE already ruled against for action.level (#1318/#1324) and progress (#2533 Fork 2). CAVEAT for the ratifier: `expected` ('planned/known drift, not built yet') classifies the NATURE of the divergence, not the review verdict — a region can be `expected` AND `unreviewed` — so it likely belongs on a THIRD `nature` axis, not folded beside accept/reject; recommend the third axis (does not block the mint). FINDING 2 — anchoring splits pixel vs structural along the 'are the two panes pixel-aligned?' line: visual-regression (baseline vs new capture of the same view) is pixel-aligned so a bounding box locates a region; design-vs-built / Figma / PR-diff panes are NOT pixel-aligned so they anchor structurally (dom-selector / node-id / line-range). The board's design→built case is the structural kind; a whole-pattern contract must carry BOTH, tagged. FINDING 3 — the board surfaced a THIRD disposition no incumbent first-classes: 'expected, not reached yet' (known drift, neither accept nor reject) — the novel contribution. FINDING 4 — the tools have a clean producer/consumer split (a DIFFER turns two renders into typed regions; a REVIEW SURFACE renders + dispositions them), mirroring Web Graph's two-seam split (CustomGraphLayout invents positions, CustomGraphRenderer draws) — so the end-state is likely a visual-diff INTENT (review surface) + a swappable differ PROTOCOL (pixelmatch/odiff/reg-cli), but THIS decision should mint the intent and only NAME the differ seam as a follow-on. SHAPED CONTRACT: (3a) delta-type taxonomy = two orthogonal axes — structural type {added|removed|changed} × review disposition {unreviewed|accepted|rejected|expected}; (3b) anchor payload = a tagged union anchorType {pixel-region|dom-selector|node-id|line-range} with box? for pixel and ref? for structural, plus the two pane refs; (3c) accept model = a per-region disposition that promotes built→baseline on accept, first-classes 'expected', and may be scoped by type, with the whole-surface approve composed ABOVE it. RECOMMENDATION: MINT a visual-diff intent on the shaped contract (not park, not mint-blind). Full grounding in we:reports/2026-07-18-annotated-visual-diff-surface.md.
